title: scaevola plumieri
intro: Scaevola plumieri, also known as the blue fan flower or fan flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of the Pacific Islands. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Scaevola plumieri include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves can also be dried and used as a natural remedy for various ailments.
